dateline: Hawthorne, CA / Boca Chica, TX — May 2002–Present
heroHeadline: Falcon Heavy Returns to Flight After 18 Months — Launches ViaSat-3 F3 to GTO; Twin RTLS Marks 604th–605th Booster Landings
heroSubtitle: 8,760 days of reusability milestones — Falcon Heavy (12th ever) returns to flight Apr 29 at 14:13 UTC from LC-39A after 18-month gap; B1072/B1075 land simultaneously at LZ-2/LZ-40 (604th–605th recoveries); ViaSat-3 F3 (6.6 t) to GTO, completes Asia-Pacific broadband constellation; SpaceX's 52nd orbital mission of 2026. IPO window now June 18–30 per Motley Fool Apr 27 timeline ($75B raise, ~$1.75T, 30% retail). Starship IFT-12 targeting May 2026.
footerNote: Data sourced from SpaceX.com, FAA, NASA, SpaceNews, NASASpaceflight.com, Spaceflight Now, Teslarati, TeslaOracle, CNBC, Bloomberg, TechCrunch, TIME, Space.com, Inside GNSS, Northrop Grumman, Yahoo Finance, Motley Fool, ARK Invest, Quilty Space, RocketLaunch.org
